content.problemStatement:
Last year, a total of 1010010^{100}10100 contests were held on CodeChef. This led to a spread of ratism in the CodeChef community.

A ratist is a person who bullies **everyone** with a **strictly lower** rating than them.
Initially, only one person is ratist in the community.

A non-ratist becomes a ratist iff:


They were bullied by a ratist in the past, and;
Their rating becomes **strictly higher** than one of the people that bullied them.
There are NNN people. The rating of the ithi^{th}ith person fluctuates in the range [Ai,Bi][A_i, B_i][Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã].

Given that only person iii is a ratist initially, find the **maximum** number of people who might have been ratists at any point in time.

You need to find the answer for each 1‚â§i‚â§N1\le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of people.
The next NNN lines contain 222 space-separated integers each - AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã representing that the rating of ithi^{th}ith person fluctuated in the interval [Ai,Bi][A_i, B_i][Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã].




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers: C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã, where CiC_iCi‚Äã is the maximum number of ratists at any point of time, if person iii was the only ratist initially.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 222 : Here is an example on how we can start with the ratist being the 1st1^{st}1st person and achieve a maximum of 222 ratists.
Let the initial ratings of everyone be [3,1,2,3][3, 1, 2, 3][3,1,2,3]. Here, since person 111 is a ratist, he is bullies person 222 and 333.
After a contest, the ratings can become [1,1,2,3][1, 1, 2, 3][1,1,2,3]. Now, person 333 who was previously bullied by person 111 has become higher rated than him and thus becomes ratist. Hence we have 222 ratists.
Do note that person 222 doesn't become ratist because his rating is equal to his bully and not strictly higher.
It can be shown that we cannot achieve more than 222 ratists after any number of contests.
